Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Complex A sophisticated cyber attack orchestrated by the 'Outer Space' campaign targets the healthcare sector with the aim of exfiltrating sensitive data and disrupting operations. Utilizing advanced techniques, the attackers meticulously plan and execute their strategy to infiltrate networks, maintain persistence, escalate privileges, evade defenses, access credentials, discover valuable resources, move laterally, collect data, exfiltrate information, and ultimately encrypt data for impact, demanding ransom.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, Application Log, Script File, Windows Registry, Network Traffic, Target Artifact (Mobile device), Logon Session, Target Artifact (Channel)
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Application Log Content (Mechanism), Web Shell, Time Providers, Reg.exe Utility, Mimikatz Tool, Network Service Scanning, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Logon Session Creation (Mechanism), Exfiltration Over C2 Channel, Data Encrypted for Impact

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: The attackers engage in active scanning activities to map out the network topology of the healthcare systems, identifying potential entry points and vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 Name: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: The attackers setup command and control servers and other necessary infrastructure to support the attack campaign.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 439 Name: Manipulation During Distribution
stage.description: The attackers compromise the software update mechanism to distribute malware, gaining initial access to the healthcare network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121 Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: The attackers exploit known vulnerabilities in public-facing health portals or applications to execute malicious code.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 650 Name: Upload a Web Shell to a Web Server
stage.description: To maintain persistent access, the attackers upload a web shell onto the compromised server.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers leverage weaknesses in time synchronization mechanisms to escalate their privileges within the network.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 203 Name: Manipulate Registry Information
stage.description: The attackers disable security tools and logging mechanisms by modifying the Windows Registry settings.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 226 Name: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: The attackers steal credentials using tools like Mimikatz to facilitate lateral movement within the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: The attackers scan the network to identify critical services and data stores.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 324 Name: TCP (ISN) Sequence Predictability Probe
stage.description: The attackers utilize remote services like RDP or SSH to move across the network undetected.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 277 Name: Data Interchange Protocol Manipulation
stage.description: The attackers extract sensitive patient data from repositories within the network.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 24 Name: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attackers transfer the collected data to attacker-controlled servers using established command and control channels.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 609 Name: Cellular Traffic Intercept
stage.description: The attackers encrypt data to disrupt operations and demand ransom, impacting the healthcare institution's ability to function.

Scenario.summary: The Outer Space campaign employs a multi-stage complex attack targeting the healthcare sector, utilizing sophisticated techniques to gain initial access, persist within the network, elevate privileges, evade detection, harvest credentials, and exfiltrate sensitive data before finally encrypting it for impact, aiming to extort financial gains.
